title: SQL Repositories, Pools, Transactions, and Request Consistency · Go Semantics Case: Request Commit Ambiguity
summary: Handlers open a new database handle per request, concatenate resource IDs, leak rows, perform multi-step writes outside transactions, and return before commit ambiguity is resolved. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo request commit ambiguity increment in context-bound repository and transaction contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Handle canceled requests and ambiguous commit results with idempotency, reconciliation, durable identifiers, and no claim unsupported by storage evidence.
Map no rows, uniqueness, foreign key, serialization, deadline, cancellation, unavailable, and unknown failures without exposing schema internals.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
